SEARCH
Module DSA
Package com.dsa.pattern

Class BitManipulation

java.lang.Object
com.dsa.pattern.BitManipulation
public class BitManipulation extends Object
Demonstrates the Bit Manipulation Pattern.

Bit Manipulation involves directly working with the binary representation of numbers to perform operations efficiently. It is commonly used for optimization and solving problems involving sets, flags, and XOR properties.

Key Idea

  • Use bitwise operators: AND (invalid input: '&'), OR (|), XOR (^), NOT (~), left shift (invalid input: '<'invalid input: '<'), right shift (>>)
  • Check, set, clear, or toggle bits efficiently
  • Use XOR for problems like finding a single non-repeated element

When to Use

  • Checking if a number is even/odd
  • Finding powers of 2
  • Counting set bits
  • Finding the only non-repeating number in an array
  • Subsets, masks, and flags

Example Implementation


 package com.dsa.pattern;

 public class BitManipulationExample {

 	// Check if nth bit is set
 	public static boolean isBitSet(int num, int n) {
 		return (num & (1 << n)) != 0;
 	}

 	// Set nth bit
 	public static int setBit(int num, int n) {
 		return num | (1 << n);
 	}

 	// Clear nth bit
 	public static int clearBit(int num, int n) {
 		return num & ~(1 << n);
 	}

 	// Toggle nth bit
 	public static int toggleBit(int num, int n) {
 		return num ^ (1 << n);
 	}

 	// Count set bits (Hamming weight)
 	public static int countSetBits(int num) {
 		int count = 0;
 		while (num != 0) {
 			num &= (num - 1); // clear the least significant set bit
 			count++;
 		}
 		return count;
 	}

 	// Find single non-repeating number in array using XOR
 	public static int singleNonRepeating(int[] arr) {
 		int result = 0;
 		for (int num : arr)
 			result ^= num;
 		return result;
 	}

 	public static void main(String[] args) {
 		int num = 21; // 10101 in binary
 		System.out.println("Is bit 2 set? " + isBitSet(num, 2));
 		System.out.println("Set bit 1: " + setBit(num, 1));
 		System.out.println("Clear bit 4: " + clearBit(num, 4));
 		System.out.println("Toggle bit 0: " + toggleBit(num, 0));

 		int[] arr = { 2, 3, 2, 4, 4 };
 		System.out.println("Single non-repeating number: " + singleNonRepeating(arr));
 	}
 }

Complexity

  • Time Complexity: O(n) for counting set bits or XOR over array of size n
  • Space Complexity: O(1)

Applications

  • Set operations using bit masks
  • Single element detection (XOR trick)
  • Subsets generation
  • Optimizations in competitive programming
  • Flags and permissions representation
